core_model.structure_summary: A planar autonomous first-order system in which each variable's rate combines a product of nonlinear functions of the two variables (entering the two equations with opposite signs) and a linear-in-that-function growth or decay term, admitting a conserved scalar first integral and a change of variables to canonical conjugate form.
core_model.canonical_form: \dot{x}_1 = a\, f_1(x_1) f_2(x_2) - b\, f_1(x_1), \quad \dot{x}_2 = -c\, f_1(x_1) f_2(x_2) + d\, f_2(x_2); \qquad H = c\,x_1 - d\,F_1(x_1) + a\,x_2 - b\,F_2(x_2), \quad F_i' = 1/f_i, \quad \dot{H}=0; \qquad \dot{y}_1 = \partial H/\partial y_2, \; \dot{y}_2 = -\partial H/\partial y_1

variables.meaning: two positive scalar quantities whose product-type coupling transfers growth from one to the other
four coefficients weighting the cross-coupling and the self growth/decay terms
two monotone functions shaping how each variable enters the coupling

deterministic_skeleton.summary: A planar first-order system with antisymmetric product coupling between the two variables plus linear self growth/decay, conserving a scalar first integral whose level sets are closed orbits around an interior equilibrium.

notes: The paper generalizes the classical bilinear antisymmetric two-variable system by composing each variable through a nonlinear function f_i; the main result identifies the unique choice (quadratic transform, phi_i(y)=2y) giving closed-form solutions in elementary functions. The conserved-integral / canonical-conjugate structure is the load-bearing framework; power-law and N-component extensions are secondary.
